verses.text: God promises to be 'the God of all the families of Israel,' declaring, 'I have loved thee with an everlasting love.' His love is the ground of all. The faithfulness of God to His people springs from a love that never began and never ends.

God promises to be 'the God of all the families of Israel,' declaring, 'I have loved thee with an everlasting love.' His love is the ground of all. The faithfulness of God to His people springs from a love that never began and never ends.

God promises to be 'the God of all the families of Israel,' declaring, 'I have loved thee with an everlasting love.' His love is the ground of all. The faithfulness of God to His people springs from a love that never began and never ends.

He will rebuild the virgin of Israel, restore her dancing and vineyards, and watchmen shall cry, 'Arise... let us go up to Zion.' Joy returns. God turns ruin into rebuilding and mourning into festival.

He will rebuild the virgin of Israel, restore her dancing and vineyards, and watchmen shall cry, 'Arise... let us go up to Zion.' Joy returns. God turns ruin into rebuilding and mourning into festival.

He will rebuild the virgin of Israel, restore her dancing and vineyards, and watchmen shall cry, 'Arise... let us go up to Zion.' Joy returns. God turns ruin into rebuilding and mourning into festival.

He will gather them from the north, even the blind and lame, leading them with weeping and supplications 'by the rivers of waters.' His care is tender. The Shepherd gathers the weakest and guides them gently home.

He will gather them from the north, even the blind and lame, leading them with weeping and supplications 'by the rivers of waters.' His care is tender. The Shepherd gathers the weakest and guides them gently home.

He will gather them from the north, even the blind and lame, leading them with weeping and supplications 'by the rivers of waters.' His care is tender. The Shepherd gathers the weakest and guides them gently home.

'He that scattered Israel will gather him,' keeping him as a shepherd his flock; they shall flow to the goodness of the LORD. He undoes the scattering. The God who dispersed in judgment regathers in mercy.

'He that scattered Israel will gather him,' keeping him as a shepherd his flock; they shall flow to the goodness of the LORD. He undoes the scattering. The God who dispersed in judgment regathers in mercy.

'He that scattered Israel will gather him,' keeping him as a shepherd his flock; they shall flow to the goodness of the LORD. He undoes the scattering. The God who dispersed in judgment regathers in mercy.
He will turn their mourning into joy and 'satiate the soul of the priests with fatness.' Sorrow is exchanged for gladness. God fills His people with good when He restores them.
He will turn their mourning into joy and 'satiate the soul of the priests with fatness.' Sorrow is exchanged for gladness. God fills His people with good when He restores them.

'A voice was heard in Ramah... Rahel weeping for her children'; yet 'there is hope in thine end,' for the children shall return. Matthew sees this at Bethlehem. Even bitter grief is met with the promise of hope.

'A voice was heard in Ramah... Rahel weeping for her children'; yet 'there is hope in thine end,' for the children shall return. Matthew sees this at Bethlehem. Even bitter grief is met with the promise of hope.

'A voice was heard in Ramah... Rahel weeping for her children'; yet 'there is hope in thine end,' for the children shall return. Matthew sees this at Bethlehem. Even bitter grief is met with the promise of hope.

God hears Ephraim's repentance and cries, 'is Ephraim my dear son?... my bowels are troubled for him; I will surely have mercy.' His heart yearns. The God offended is moved with tenderness toward His penitent child.

God hears Ephraim's repentance and cries, 'is Ephraim my dear son?... my bowels are troubled for him; I will surely have mercy.' His heart yearns. The God offended is moved with tenderness toward His penitent child.

He bids her set up waymarks and return, asking how long she will wander; 'the LORD hath created a new thing.' Return is urged. God marks the road home and calls the backslider to come.
He bids her set up waymarks and return, asking how long she will wander; 'the LORD hath created a new thing.' Return is urged. God marks the road home and calls the backslider to come.
Men shall again bless 'the habitation of justice,' for God has 'satiated the weary soul.' Refreshment is promised. The God of restoration revives those worn out with sorrow.

He will sow the land afresh, and each shall die for his own sin, no more the proverb of children's teeth set on edge by fathers. Justice is personal. Under God's renewing each answers for himself.
He will sow the land afresh, and each shall die for his own sin, no more the proverb of children's teeth set on edge by fathers. Justice is personal. Under God's renewing each answers for himself.

'I will make a new covenant... I will put my law in their inward parts, and write it in their hearts... I will forgive their iniquity.' Hebrews shows this fulfilled in Christ. The new covenant gives an inward law and full pardon.

'I will make a new covenant... I will put my law in their inward parts, and write it in their hearts... I will forgive their iniquity.' Hebrews shows this fulfilled in Christ. The new covenant gives an inward law and full pardon.

As surely as the sun, moon, and stars keep their courses, so sure is God's covenant with Israel; only if those depart will His people be cast off. His promise is fixed. The faithfulness of God is as constant as the heavens.

As surely as the sun, moon, and stars keep their courses, so sure is God's covenant with Israel; only if those depart will His people be cast off. His promise is fixed. The faithfulness of God is as constant as the heavens.

As surely as the sun, moon, and stars keep their courses, so sure is God's covenant with Israel; only if those depart will His people be cast off. His promise is fixed. The faithfulness of God is as constant as the heavens.
The city shall be rebuilt to the LORD, holy, and 'not be plucked up... any more for ever.' Permanence is promised. God's restored dwelling shall endure beyond all overthrow.
